specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI Responses API streaming handler processes named SSE events from the Responses API endpoint and translates them into normalized stream events, handling text output, reasoning summaries, tool calls, and completion.

scope.in-scope: Named Responses API event dispatch (response.created, response.output_text.delta, response.reasoning_summary_text.delta, response.output_item.added, response.function_call_arguments.delta, response.output_item.done, error), model name caching, and tool argument accumulation.
scope.boundary: Which models use the Responses API versus Chat Completions is covered by the OpenAI API routing and reasoning effort specifications.
How requests are serialized for the Responses API is covered by the provider serialization specification.

behavior.response-created: The `response.created` event contains a model field. The model name is extracted and cached for use in the completion record.
behavior.text-delta: The `response.output_text.delta` event contains a delta field with the partial text output. The delta is emitted as a TEXT_DELTA stream event. An optional content_index field identifies the output item position but is not used in routing.
behavior.reasoning-delta: The `response.reasoning_summary_text.delta` event contains a delta field with partial reasoning (thinking) output. The delta is emitted as a THINKING_DELTA stream event. An optional summary_index field is present but not used in routing.
behavior.output-item-added:
The `response.output_item.added` event signals that a new output item has started. For items of type `function_call`: - Any previously open tool call is ended. - The accumulated arguments string is reset to empty. - The call ID and function name are extracted from the item. - A TOOL_CALL_START event is emitted with the call ID and function name.
Items of other types (such as text) are ignored; their content arrives through the output_text.delta event.
behavior.function-call-arguments-delta: The `response.function_call_arguments.delta` event carries a partial arguments string for the current tool call. The delta is appended to the accumulated arguments string. A TOOL_CALL_DELTA event is emitted carrying the delta text (not the full accumulated string).
behavior.output-item-done:
The `response.output_item.done` event signals that an output item is complete. For function_call items, a TOOL_CALL_DONE event is emitted. The tool call active flag is cleared, but the tool call data (ID, name, accumulated arguments) is preserved for use in the completion response builder.
Items of other types are ignored.
behavior.error-event:
The `error` event contains a type and message. The error type string is mapped to an error category: - "authentication_error" → AUTH - "rate_limit_error" → RATE_LIMIT - "invalid_request_error" → INVALID_ARG - "server_error" → SERVER - All other values → UNKNOWN
An ERROR stream event is emitted with the category and message.
behavior.notable: Tool call arguments arrive in deltas and are accumulated by concatenation. The TOOL_CALL_DELTA event carries each individual delta for the REPL layer to use, while the internal accumulator builds the complete arguments string for the completion record.
